# terrible_cisco_intersight_intersight_network_connectivity_policy (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) The name assigned to the Network Connectivity Policy. The name must be between 1 and 62 alphanumeric characters, allowing special characters :-_.

### Optional

- `alternate_ipv4_dns_server` (String) IP address of the secondary DNS server. This is used when enable_ipv4_dns_from_dhcp is false.
- `alternate_ipv6_dns_server` (String) IP address of the secondary DNS server.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) The user-defined description for the Network Connectivity Policy. Description can contain letters(a-z, A-Z), numbers(0-9), hyphen(-), period(.), colon(:), or an underscore(_).
- `dynamic_dns_domain` (String) The domain name appended to a hostname for a Dynamic DNS (DDNS) update. If left blank, only a hostname is sent to the DDNS update request. This parameter is optional even when enable_dynamic_dns is true.
- `enable_dynamic_dns` (Boolean) If enabled, updates the resource records to the DNS from Cisco IMC.
- `enable_ipv4_dns_from_dhcp` (Boolean) Enable IPv4 DNS from DHCP. If enabled, Cisco IMC retrieves the DNS server addresses from DHCP.
- `enable_ipv6` (Boolean) If enabled, allows to configure IPv6 properties.
- `enable_ipv6_dns_from_dhcp` (Boolean) If enabled, Cisco IMC retrieves the DNS server addresses from DHCP. Use DHCP field must be enabled for IPv6 in Cisco IMC to enable it.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `organization` (String) The name of the Organization this resource is assigned to. Profiles, Policies, and Pools that are created within a Custom Organization are applicable only to devices in the same Organization.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `preferred_ipv4_dns_server` (String) IP address of the primary DNS server. This is used when enable_ipv4_dns_from_dhcp is false.
- `preferred_ipv6_dns_server` (String) IP address of the primary DNS server.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) If `present`, will verify the resource is present and will create if needed. If `absent`, will verify the resource is absent and will delete if needed.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `api_response` (String) The API response output returned by the specified resource.
- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
